title: Diego and the Lost Baby Bird
synopsis: When Diego finds a scared baby bird that has fallen from its nest, he embarks on a brave adventure through the park to help it find its mama. Along the way, he meets helpful friends and learns that being brave means helping others even when you feel a little scared yourself.

pages.text: Diego was playing in the sunny park with his abuela. He loved running on the soft green grass and listening to the birds sing in the tall trees. Diego had curly black hair and wore his favorite red shirt with a dinosaur on it.

illustration.description: A happy Latino boy with curly black hair wearing a bright red dinosaur t-shirt running through a lush green park. His grandmother, a warm-looking Latina woman with gray hair in a colorful floral dress, watches him with a smile. Tall trees with full green leaves frame the scene, and birds can be seen in the branches. The style is cheerful cartoon with bold primary colors and rounded, friendly shapes.
illustration.alt: Diego playing in the park with his grandmother

pages.text: Suddenly, Diego heard a tiny "peep peep!" He looked down and saw a small yellow baby bird on the ground. The little bird looked scared and was shaking. Its tiny wings fluttered, but it couldn't fly up to the trees.

illustration.description: Diego kneeling down on the grass, looking concerned at a small yellow baby bird with fluffy feathers sitting on the ground. The bird has big round eyes and looks frightened, with its tiny wings slightly spread. Diego's expression shows worry and care. The background shows green grass with dappled sunlight, creating a gentle, warm atmosphere in cartoon style with soft textures.
illustration.alt: Diego finding a scared baby bird on the ground

pages.text: "Oh no, little bird!" said Diego softly. "Are you lost?" The baby bird peeped sadly. Diego felt sad too when he saw how scared the little bird was. He wanted to help, but he wasn't sure what to do.

illustration.description: A close-up of Diego's caring face as he gently reaches toward the baby bird with both hands cupped. His expression shows empathy and determination. The yellow baby bird looks up at him with hope in its big round eyes. Soft golden sunlight filters through tree leaves above them, creating a warm, tender moment. The illustration style emphasizes rounded shapes and expressive facial features.
illustration.alt: Diego gently approaching the sad baby bird

pages.text: Diego carefully picked up the baby bird in his gentle hands. "Don't worry, little friend. I will help you find your mama!" he said bravely. Even though Diego felt a little scared about the big adventure, he wanted to be brave for the baby bird.

illustration.description: Diego standing confidently with the baby bird safely cupped in his hands close to his chest. His expression shows determination and bravery, with his shoulders squared and chin raised slightly. The baby bird looks more comfortable now, nestled securely in Diego's protective hands. Behind them, the park stretches out with winding paths and colorful flowers, suggesting the adventure ahead.
illustration.alt: Diego bravely holding the baby bird, ready to help

pages.text: Diego walked around the park looking up at all the trees. He met Mrs. Chen, who was feeding ducks at the pond. "Have you seen a mama bird looking for her baby?" asked Diego. Mrs. Chen smiled and pointed to the big oak tree. "I heard chirping over there!"

illustration.description: Diego talking to a kind Asian woman with short black hair wearing a purple jacket, standing by a peaceful pond with ducks swimming. She's pointing toward a large oak tree in the distance. Diego holds the baby bird carefully while looking hopeful. The scene includes other diverse families in the background enjoying the park, with bright blue water and green surroundings creating a welcoming community atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Diego asking Mrs. Chen for help finding the mama bird

pages.text: At the big oak tree, Diego heard loud chirping from high up in the branches. "Tweet tweet tweet!" called a mama bird. She sounded worried and sad. The baby bird in Diego's hands started peeping excitedly. "Peep peep peep!" They had found each other!

illustration.description: Diego looking up at a magnificent oak tree with thick branches and dense green leaves. A brown mama bird is visible on a branch, leaning forward with her beak open as she calls down. Diego's face shows joy and excitement as he holds up the baby bird, whose wings are fluttering happily. The tree dominates the scene with dappled sunlight creating a magical, hopeful atmosphere through the leaves.
illustration.alt: Diego and the baby bird hearing mama bird in the oak tree

pages.text: But how could Diego get the baby bird back up to its mama? The tree was so tall! Just then, his friend Marcus came running over with his dad. Marcus was a tall boy with dark skin who loved to climb trees. "I can help!" Marcus said bravely.

illustration.description: Marcus, a tall Black boy with short hair wearing a green t-shirt, running toward Diego with his father, a Black man in casual clothes with a warm smile. Diego looks relieved and happy to see his friend. The massive oak tree looms behind them, emphasizing how tall it is. The composition shows friendship and community support with bright, encouraging colors and dynamic movement.
illustration.alt: Marcus and his dad arriving to help Diego

pages.text: Marcus's dad lifted Marcus up high, and Marcus carefully placed the baby bird on a low branch. The mama bird flew down right away! She chirped happily and nuzzled her baby with her beak. The baby bird was so happy to be with his mama again.

illustration.description: Marcus being lifted up by his dad to place the baby bird on a tree branch, while Diego watches with joy from below. The mama bird is flying down with outstretched wings to reunite with her baby. Both birds look ecstatic, with the baby bird fluffing up its feathers in excitement. The scene captures a moment of pure happiness and successful rescue, with warm golden light filtering through the tree.
illustration.alt: Marcus helping reunite the baby bird with its mama

pages.text: Diego felt so happy and proud! He had been brave and helped the little bird find its family. Abuela gave him a big hug. "You did a wonderful thing, mijo," she said. Diego smiled as he watched the bird family fly away together, chirping joyfully in the bright blue sky.

illustration.description: Diego beaming with pride as his abuela hugs him tightly, both looking up at the sky where the mama and baby bird are flying together among fluffy white clouds. Marcus and his dad stand nearby, also smiling and watching the birds. The scene radiates warmth and accomplishment, with a brilliant blue sky and the suggestion of a perfect ending. Everyone's faces show pure joy and satisfaction.
illustration.alt: Diego feeling proud and happy as the bird family flies away together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Diego feel when he first saw the baby bird?
questions.explanation: Diego felt sad when he saw how scared the little bird was.

questions.question: Who helped Diego find the mama bird?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen was feeding ducks and pointed Diego toward the big oak tree where she heard chirping.

questions.question: How did the baby bird get back to its mama?
choices: Marcus put it on a branch
questions.explanation: Marcus's dad lifted Marcus up, and Marcus carefully placed the baby bird on a low branch.

questions.question: How did Diego feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Diego felt happy and proud because he had been brave and helped the little bird find its family.

sentences.text: Diego found a scared little _____ on the ground.
Diego felt _____ when he wanted to help the baby bird.
The mama bird was _____ in the tall oak tree.
At the end, Diego felt _____ and proud.
